zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington於1335年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾巴金哈姆時，他的父親Sir William Smythe of Carrington25歲，母親Lady Catherine Montague20歲。他於1355年與Lady Margaret de Ros在英國艾可利文哈爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1378年在英國英格蘭米德萊可享年43歲。

nameMeanings.name: Smythe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Smith
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name denoting a worker in metal, especially iron, such as a blacksmith or farrier, from Middle English smith ‘smith’ (Old English smith, probably a derivative of smītan ‘to strike, hammer’). Early examples are also found in the Latin form Faber . Metal-working was one of the earliest occupations for which specialist skills were required, and its importance ensured that this term and its equivalents in other languages were the most widespread of all occupational surnames in Europe. Medieval smiths were important not only in making horseshoes, plowshares, and other domestic articles, but above all for their skill in forging swords, other weapons, and armor. This is also the most frequent of all surnames in the US. It is very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below). This surname (in any of the two possible English senses; see also below) is also found in Haiti. See also Smither .

English: from Middle English smithe ‘smithy, forge’ (Old English smiththe). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived in or by a blacksmith's shop, occupational, for someone who worked in one, or habitational, from a place so named, such as Smitha in King's Nympton (Devon). Compare Smithey .
Irish and Scottish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac Gobhann, Irish Mac Gabhann ‘son of the smith’. See McGowan .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington was born in 1335, in Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William Smythe of Carrington, was 25 and his mother, Lady Catherine Montague, was 20. He married Lady Margaret de Ros in 1355, in Rivenhall, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1378, in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington est né en 1335, à Buckingham, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir William Smythe of Carrington, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Lady Catherine Montague, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret de Ros en 1355, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1378, à Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans.

Er hat 1355 in Rivenhall, Essex, England Lady Margaret de Ros geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1378 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Middlesex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington(이)가 1335년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 벅잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir William Smythe of Carrington(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Catherine Montague(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1355년 영국 에세스 라이븐홀에서 Lady Margaret de Ros(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1378년 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.
